title: HTML and CSS Frameworks Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the different types of frameworks used to speed up web development and ensure visual consistency.

questions.question: Which of these frameworks is considered 'Utility-First'?
options: Bootstrap
Foundation
Tailwind CSS
Bulma

questions.question: What is a primary 'Pro' of using a CSS framework like Bootstrap?
options: It makes your HTML file smaller.
It provides a collection of pre-designed, responsive components (like buttons and navbars) ready to use.
It automatically translates your content into multiple languages.
It completely replaces the need for JavaScript.

questions.question: Which of these is a possible 'Con' of using a heavy CSS framework for a small project?
options: The project will load much faster.
Your site will always look unique compared to others.
Potentially large file sizes due to unused CSS rules.
It is always more difficult to learn than raw CSS.

questions.question: In the context of modern React development, what does 'CSS-in-JS' refer to?
options: Writing CSS in a separate .css file.
Writing your CSS rules directly within your JavaScript component files.
Using JavaScript to download CSS files from a CDN.
A way to convert CSS into a JSON object.

questions.question: Before choosing a heavy framework, what native CSS tools should you consider for your layout needs?
options: Floats and Clearfixes
Table-based layouts
Flexbox and CSS Grid
Framesets
